commands.name: create, generate, n, new
commands.description: Creates a new project based on the selected template if it exists

options.name: --id
options.description: fully.qualified.name of the template, e.g. perla.templates.vanilla.js []

options.name: --template
options.description: shortname of the template, e.g. ff []

options.name: --skip
options.description: Skip interactive prompts and use defaults [default: False]

commands.name: r, restore
commands.description: Restores the project dependencies from the perla.json file

options.name: --source
options.description: The source to download packages from. Defaults to jspm.io [default: ValueNone]

commands.name: a, add, i, install
commands.description: Adds a package to the project dependencies

arguments.name: PACKAGES
arguments.description: set of packages to add as dependencies

commands.name: remove, rm
commands.description: Removes a package from the project dependencies

arguments.name: PACKAGES
arguments.description: set of packages to add as dependencies

commands.name: list, ls
commands.description: Lists the current dependencies in a table or an npm style json string

options.name: --as-package-json
options.description: Show the packages similar to npm's package.json []

commands.name: s, serve, start
commands.description: Starts the development server and if fable projects are present it also takes care of it.

commands.name: b, build
commands.description: Builds the SPA application for distribution

options.name: --preview
options.description: Enable preview mode, which will build the application and start a static server []

commands.name: templates
commands.description: Handles Template Repository operations such as list, add, update, and remove templates

options.name: --add
options.description: If it doesn't exist, adds the template repository to Perla []

options.name: --update
options.description: If it exists, updates the template repository for Perla []

options.name: --remove
options.description: If it exists, removes the template repository for Perla []

options.name: --list-format
options.description: The chosen format to display the existing templates [default: HumanReadable]

commands.name: describe, ds
commands.description: Describes the perla.json file or it's properties as requested

options.name: --current
options.description: Take my current perla.json file and print my current configuration

arguments.name: PROPERTIES
arguments.description: A property, properties or json path-like string names to describe
